title: pinguicula vulgaris subsp. alpicola
intro: Pinguicula vulgaris subsp. alpicola, also known as the alpine butterwort, is a carnivorous plant species belonging to the family Lentibulariaceae. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Pinguicula vulgaris subsp. alpicola include soothing sore throats, relieving congestion, and reducing inflammation in the body. In herbal preparations, the leaves of the plant are typically harvested and dried to make tea or infusions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
